Japanese: 美柳　ちなみにとって‥‥ どうしてもジャマな人物がいた。
English: There was one person that was standing squarely in Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne's way...

Japanese: それは、あなたよ！ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: And that person was... Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ぼぼぼぼぼボクですってェッ‥‥！
English: M-M-M-M-M-M-Meeee!?

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥ ムチャクチャですぞ！
English: Th-That's preposterous!!

Japanese: だいいち、亡くなったのは 呑田　菊三くんでしょうがッ！
English: After all, it was Doug Swallow that was murdered!

Japanese: たしかに、結果的には そうなりましたが‥‥
English: Well, it's true that that's how things worked out...

Japanese: でも‥‥呑田さんの死因は感電。 毒薬はカンケイありません。
English: But, let's remember that Mr. Swallow died of electrocution, not poison.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが 毒殺をたくらんだ人物は‥‥
English: The person that Ms. Hawthorne was planning to poison was in fact...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥なるほどくん。 あなたしか考えられないの。
English: You, Phoenix Wright. There's no one else that it could be.

Japanese: し、しかし！　成歩堂くんは‥‥、 ちなみさんのコイビトですぞ！
English: B-But how can that be!? I-I thought Mr. Wright and Ms. Hawthorne were in love!

Japanese: （ツラいでしょうね。 ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥）
English: (Poor Mr. Wright... This must be killing him...)

Japanese: （でも、ガマンして。 カタキはとるわ。‥‥かならず）
English: (Hang in there... I'll bring her to justice... I swear it!)

Japanese: ‥‥先ほども言いましたが。
English: As I said before, the only thing Ms. Hawthorne truly cared about was...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみにとって ダイジだったのは‥‥
English: ...the one piece of evidence linking her to that incident eight months ago.

Japanese: 殺人の証拠品‥‥あのペンダント だけだったのです！
English: That's right. The bottle necklace. That's all she cared about.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥なぜですか！ サツジンなどと‥‥！
English: But even so... Why... Why would she go so far as to murder him!?

Japanese: ‥‥半年前。地下食堂で 被害者の弁護士が倒れた直後‥‥
English: Eight months ago, just after the fall of that attorney in the basement cafeteria...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが考えていた ことは、ただ１つでした。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne could think of only one thing.

Japanese: 《毒薬が入ったペンダントを 一刻も早く、始末すること》‥‥
English: How to get rid of the bottle necklace as quickly as possible!

Japanese: ‥‥う、ウソだ‥‥
English: N-No... It-It can't be...

Japanese: 彼女の見込みは正しかった。 “証拠品”は、守られました。
English: It was a pretty good move she made, too. The evidence was missing for a long time.

Japanese: しかし‥‥ここで、 大きな問題が発生した。
English: But there was just one big problem.

Japanese: 事件のほとぼりがさめても、彼が それを返そうとしなかったのです！
English: Although she got him to hide the evidence, Mr. Wright refused to return it to her.

Japanese: あろうことか、 それを《宝物》にして‥‥
English: To him, the tiny little bottle was a cherished treasure.

Japanese: 会うひと、みんなに 見せびらかしていた！
English: He even showed it to everyone he met!

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ そのために、成歩堂くんを‥‥？
English: Y-You mean... TH-THAT'S why she tried to kill Mr. Wright...?

Japanese: 殺害して、取り戻そうとした。 ‥‥それだけのことです。
English: Correct, Your Honor. It was to retrieve that piece of evidence.
Th-Th-Th...

Japanese: ウソだわわああああああぁぁぁん！
English: That can't be trueeeeeeeee!!

Japanese: ちょっと、ジャマよ。 どきなさいな‥‥リュウちゃん。
English: "Feenie"... What a joke you are.

Japanese: ほんッと、宇宙のはてまで たよりにならないオトコ。
English: Honestly, how can any woman ever count on you for anything?

Japanese: あれほど、わたしのコトは だまっておけ、って言ったのに。
English: I even told you time and time again to keep your trap shut about me and that necklace.

Japanese: ‥‥クズがッ！
English: ...You disgust me!

Japanese: しょ、しょ‥‥証人‥‥？
English: M-Ms. Hawthorne...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥この裁判も、 終わりが近づいてきたようね。
English: It appears that this trial will be coming to an end soon...

Japanese: どうあっても、このわたしを 殺人犯にしたいみたいね。
English: Fine. I can tell you plan on making me into a criminal no matter what I say!

Japanese: あなたがやったのよ。 ‥‥美柳　ちなみ。
English: You ARE a criminal, Ms. Hawthorne!

Japanese: どうかしらね。 ‥‥証拠はどこにあるの。
English: We'll see about that. But first, where's your evidence?

Japanese: あのペンダントは、泣き虫ボウヤの おやつになっちゃったのよ？
English: It seems your sniveling little crybaby of a client has eaten the bottle as a snack.

Japanese: う‥‥。そ、それは‥‥
English: Urk...! W-Well, umm...

Japanese: ちょっと、ジイさん！ トボけたツラで座ってないで！
English: Hey! Old man! Are you senile or something!?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタも なんとか言ったらどうなのよ。
English: Why don't you say something instead of sitting there with that dumb look on your face!

Japanese: しょ、証人！ あなたは、いったい‥‥
English: M-M-Ms. Hawthorne! What's happened to you...!?

Japanese: ハン！　‥‥ナニを おどろいてるんだか‥‥。
English: Hmph! Are you really that shocked?

Japanese: こちらのほうが お気にめしまして？　オジサマ。
English: Or do you prefer me... this way, Mr. Judge?

Japanese: うむぐぐぐ‥‥！
English: Nnnnggghh...!

Japanese: 証拠もなく、善意の証人を 殺人鬼あつかい‥‥
English: With absolutely no proof, you treat a voluntary witness like she's a mass-murderer...

Japanese: ‥‥おハナシになりませんわ。 わたし、帰らせてもらいますね。
English: Well, I have nothing more to say. I'll be heading home now, if you don't mind.

Japanese: い、いや。しかし‥‥
English: B-But y-you're not finished...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ。そこのイキがった おばさまに、処分をよろしく。
English: Fine! Then ask this nasty old hag to finish up already!

Japanese: （このまま逃がすワケには‥‥！）
English: (I can't let her get away this time!)

Japanese: ‥‥ダメぢゃ、千尋クン！
English: Stop, Mia!

Japanese: 証拠もなく、これ以上 食い下がったら‥‥
English: If you keep on pushing without any evidence...

Japanese: チミは、サイアクの処分を 受けることになるぞ！
English: ...you could pay the ultimate price as a lawyer!

Japanese: サイアクの‥‥処分？
English: The ultimate price...?

Japanese: ‥‥二度と弁護士バッジを つけられなくなるでしょうな。
English: You'd be forced to take off your attorney's badge forever, I'm afraid.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
よおく考えるコトね。 ‥‥おばさま‥‥
English: You'd better think it over carefully, Ms. Fey... Or should I say, Ms. Gray.

Japanese: いかがですかな？　弁護人。
English: Well, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: 彼女の犯行を立証する、決定的な 証拠を‥‥提示できますかな？
English: Can you provide evidence that would establish her guilt once and for all?

Japanese: （ここでミスをすれば‥‥ 私の弁護士生命は、オシマイ）
English: (If I mess up here, my career as a lawyer is over!)

Japanese: （正直なところ‥‥今の時点で 決定的な証拠は、ないわ）
English: (But to be honest... at this point I don't have any evidence that's well-founded.)
(Even so...)

Japanese: （逃げるくらいなら、弁護士バッジ なんて、捨てたほうがマシよ！）
English: (I'd rather lose my attorney's badge than let her get away with murder!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　弁護側は、 証拠を提示したいと思います！
English: Your Honor! The defense would like to present proof!

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥！ できるワケがない！
English: Im-Impossible! You can't possibly...

Japanese: ‥‥バカなオンナ‥‥
English: Stupid woman!

Japanese: すでに、ギロンはじゅうぶんである ‥‥私は、そう考えます。
English: It is the opinion of the court that there has already been enough discussion.

Japanese: したがって‥‥証拠品の提示を、 １つだけ、許可しましょう。
English: Therefore, I will allow only one piece of evidence to be presented.

Japanese: ひ、１つだけ‥‥！
English: J-Just one!?

Japanese: それで、この証人の犯行が 立証できなかった場合‥‥
English: If you are unable to establish her guilt...

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一に対する判決は ゆるぎないものになるでしょう。
English: ...then I'm afraid that a very harsh verdict will immediately be handed down on Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: I understand, Your Honor.

Japanese: おばさま。ちなみが しっかり、拝見しておりますわ。
English: I can just imagine the headlines for tomorrow's newspaper.

Japanese: 花を咲かせるコトもなく、 ミゴト散りゆくサマを‥‥
English: Up-and-coming lawyer plummets to Earth before she gets the chance to soar...

Japanese: （彼女は、成歩堂　龍一の 毒殺をたくらんでいた‥‥）
English: (She was planning to poison Mr. Wright...)

Japanese: （それならば！　毒はたぶん、 アレに仕込まれていたはず！）
English: (If that's the case, then the poison was probably in there!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥提示してください。
English: Well then, Ms. Fey. Please present your evidence.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが毒殺をたくらんだ ことを、完全に立証する証拠とは！
English: Show to this court irrefutable proof that Ms. Hawthorne was planning to poison Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 弁護人‥‥。これが あなたの“コタエ”ですかッ！
English: Ms. Fey...! After all that thinking, THIS is your best answer!?

Japanese: も‥‥もう少しマシなヤツは なかったのかのお‥‥？
English: C-Couldn't you have tried a little bit harder...?

Japanese: お、お待ちください！ 今のは、ちょっと手がすべって‥‥
English: Please wait, Your Honor! I just... made a mistake!

Japanese: 言いわけ無用。 ‥‥私のカオも三度まで、ですぞ！
English: Very well. But even I have my limits!

Japanese: これが‥‥最後の、そして 決定的な証拠です。
English: Here it is, Your Honor! The evidence that will prove her guilt once and for all.

Japanese: カゼゴロシ・Ｚ‥‥ 成歩堂くんのカゼ薬、ですか。
English: Coldkiller X... Phoenix Wright's beloved cold medicine.

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥新米弁護人どのは カゼぎみ、ですかな？
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee... Does our rookie defense attorney have a bit of a cold?

Japanese: もし、私がカゼをひいても‥‥ この薬だけは飲まないでしょうね。
English: If I did, I still wouldn't take this cold medicine.

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥ 猛毒が入っていますから。
English: After all... It's been poisoned.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: 被告人の証言を 思い出してください。
English: Remember what the defendant said in his testimony.

Japanese: 事件のあった日のおヒルごはんの ときから、なくなっちゃって。
English: But I lost my bottle of it around lunchtime on the day of the accident.

Japanese: おヒルはいつも、ちいちゃんと ２人っきりで食べるんですよ。
English: I always eat with Dollie... Just the two of us.

Japanese: 被告人のカゼゴロシ・Ｚは 彼女に盗まれたのです。
English: She was the one who took his bottle of Coldkiller X.

Japanese: 猛毒を仕込んで、成歩堂　龍一に 飲ませるために！
English: Then she poisoned it, knowing that Mr. Wright was going to take some.

Japanese: ば、バカなことを‥‥！
English: Now you're really grasping at straws!

Japanese: そのビンを持っていたのは、 被害者の呑田　菊三ですぞッ！
English: After all, it was the victim, Doug Swallow, that was holding the medicine.

Japanese: みなさん。‥‥半年前の事件を 思い出してください。
English: I would like the court to recall the crime that happened here eight months ago.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが、 どこに証拠をかくしたか‥‥？
English: Where did Ms. Hawthorne hide the evidence?

Japanese: ど、どういうコトですかな？
English: Huh? What are you talking about?

Japanese: 半年前‥‥毒薬は、ペンダントの 中に入っていました。
English: Eight months ago, the poison was hidden in her bottle necklace...

Japanese: 彼女は‥‥それを、 他人に押しつけてしまったのです。
English: ...which she then gave to someone else for safekeeping.

Japanese: たまたま資料室で会った青年、 成歩堂　龍一に！
English: Someone she had accidentally run into in the reading room. My client, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: そう‥‥。 今回も、同じでした。
English: Yes, that's right... She did the same thing this time as well.

Japanese: 被害者を電柱にたたきつけた 成歩堂　龍一は、現場から去った。
English: After shoving the victim, Mr. Phoenix Wright left the scene of the crime.

Japanese: そこに現れたのが‥‥ 美柳　ちなみでした。
English: That is when the murderer, Dahlia Hawthorne, appeared.

Japanese: 彼女は、毒入りのカゼゴロシ・Ｚを 持っていた。
English: With her, she was carrying the poisoned bottle of Coldkiller X.

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、成歩堂　龍一を 毒殺するために‥‥。
English: This, of course, was so she could carry out her plan to murder Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 被告人に会いに行くところだった ‥‥そう証言していましたな。
English: Hmm, I believe she did testify that she was going to meet with the defendant.

Japanese: 彼女は現場で、すべてを聞き、 そして‥‥見ていた。
English: Yes, and she heard and saw everything that happened at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 被告人と被害者のケンカの内容も、 送電線が切れたのも。
English: Including what the defendant and victim were arguing about, and the cut electrical cable.

Japanese: “呑田　菊三を生かして おいては、あぶない‥‥！”
English: That's when she realized, "I can't allow Doug Swallow to live!"

Japanese: ‥‥彼女は送電線を使って、 呑田　菊三の口をふさいだのです。
English: She used the severed electrical cable to silence him forever.

Japanese: ところが‥‥ここで、 困ったことが起こりました。
English: Unfortunately for her, this is when the problem occurred.

Japanese: 立ち去ったはずの成歩堂　龍一が、 現場に戻ってきたのです。
English: Mr. Wright, who she thought had left the scene, came back to check on the victim.

Japanese: さらに、実験機械が止まったため、 薬学部の学生たちまで現れた。
English: And on top of that, because of the power outage, some students showed up as well.

Japanese: 彼女はアセったはずです。
English: It's hardly any wonder that she was, as she put it, in a state of panic.

Japanese: なにしろ、猛毒入りのカゼ薬を かくし持っていたのですから。
English: Recall that she was carrying that bottle of poisoned cold medicine.

Japanese: “もし、身体検査をされたら？” 半年前と、同じ状況‥‥
English: She must have thought, "What if they search me like they did eight months ago?"

Japanese: は、半年前‥‥
English: E-Eight months ago...?

Japanese: 彼女は‥‥あのときと同じ方法で 毒薬を始末したのです！
English: Yes, she disposed of the evidence exactly the same way as she did back then!

Japanese: 他人に押しつけた！ ‥‥呑田　菊三の、手の中に！
English: She had someone else hold it! In this case... Doug Swallow!

Japanese: どうしたのかしら？　みなさん。 ダマりこんじゃって。
English: Oh come on now, everyone. Surely you aren't fooled, are you?

Japanese: ‥‥こんなの、バカなオンナの 妄想に決まってるじゃない。
English: This stupid woman! She's nothing but a filthy, stinking liar!

Japanese: ‥‥ねえ、オジサマ‥‥？
English: Right, Mr. Prosecutor...?

Japanese: え‥‥ッ！
‥‥そ、そのとおりですな。 苦しまぎれの、逃げ口上‥‥
English: Y-Yes... Th-That's exactly right. It's just pure desperation!

Japanese: “苦しまぎれの逃げ口上”‥‥ 果たして、どちらのことかしら。
English: Hmm... I wonder which one of us is the desperate one?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、ちなみさん。
English: So, Ms. Hawthorne.

Japanese: この薬‥‥ あなた、飲めるかしら？
English: This cold medicine... I wonder if you wouldn't mind taking some?

Japanese: なるほどくんは あのペンダントを食べたわ。
English: Well, Mr. Wright ate that necklace of yours, right?

Japanese: あなたも試してみる？ 運がよければ‥‥助かるかもね。
English: Now it's your turn to prove your innocence. What do you say?

Japanese: もし、あなたが無実だと 主張するのならば‥‥
English: If I'm just a filthy, stinking liar, then there's no need to worry.

Japanese: たった今、それを証明しなさい！ ‥‥この薬を、飲みくだしてッ！
English: So come on! Show us! I dare you to take some of this medicine right now!

Japanese: ‥‥ぐ‥‥ぐぐううう‥‥‥‥
English: ...Grr... Nngghh... Hnnn...

Japanese: アヤサト　チヒロ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ アヤサト　チヒロオオォォ‥‥‥
English: MIA FEY...! MIA... FEYYYY...!!

Japanese: ‥‥コレデ ワタシニ勝ッタツモリ？
English: Do. You. Think. You've. Won? Well!? Do you, Mia Fey?

Japanese: ク‥‥クク‥‥クククク‥‥ ‥‥イイワ‥‥オバサマ！
English: Heh... Heh... Heh... Heheh... That's. Just. Fine!

Japanese: 今日ノトコロハ‥‥
English: For the time being...

Japanese: 今日のところは 花を持たせてあげますわ。
English: For the time being, victory is yours.

Japanese: きょ‥‥ “今日のところは”‥‥？
English: "For the time being"?

Japanese: わたし‥‥このままでは 終わりませんわよ。
English: Well... I have a very long memory, you know.

Japanese: また、いつか。 お会いするわ‥‥かならず。
English: You and I will meet again... I'm certain of it.

Japanese: それでは、おじさま‥‥。 また遊んでくださいませね。
English: Well then, Mr. Judge... I'll see you later too, OK?

Japanese: え！　‥‥は、はあ。 よろしく‥‥
English: Huh...!? Err, why, um... Y-Yes...

Japanese: じゃ‥‥わたし、おまわりさまの もとへ参りますので。
English: I'm going to go spend a little quality time with the men in blue now.

Japanese: ごきげんよろしゅう‥‥みなさま。
English: I wish you all the best.

Japanese: （これで‥‥）
English: (Whew...)

Japanese: （やっと、終わったのね‥‥）
English: (It's finally all over.)

Japanese: こ‥‥こんなバカな話‥‥ 私は認めんぞッ！ I...
English: I refuse to accept this!

Japanese: 弁護人の主張には、 なんらコンキョがないッ！
English: The defense hasn't shown a scrap of evidence to support their outrageous claim!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥もう、 本人も認めているのですよ？
English: B-But even so, your witness seems to have accepted it...

Japanese: そ、そんなコトはカンケイない！ この、亜内が！　亜内　武文が‥‥
English: I don't care!! I'm Winston Payne!

Japanese: こんな新米弁護士に！ あってはならないコトなのだッ！
English: And I don't believe one word that this rookie lawyer has said!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥亜内検事さん。 あなたにお聞きします。
English: Well then, Mr. Payne, let me ask you this.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだッ！
English: Y-Yes!?

Japanese: このクスリ‥‥飲めますか？
English: Would you care to try this cold medicine?
WHAT!?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥、さっき私に、 こう言いましたね。
English: Just a little earlier, I could've sworn you said...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。いいですかな、 新米弁護士さん。
English: There, there... It's alright, rookie.

Japanese: 私は‥‥証人・美柳　ちなみさんを イノチをかけて信頼している！
English: For example, I would trust the witness, Ms. Hawthorne, with my very life!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが、本当に 彼女を信じているのなら‥‥
English: So, if she's so trustworthy...

Japanese: この薬に、毒など入っている はずがない‥‥そうでしょう？
English: ...then I'm sure there couldn't possibly be any poison in here, right?

Japanese: う‥‥むむ‥‥それは‥‥そうかも しれないようなそうでもないような
English: Err... Well... Ummm... You see... Umm... Y-Yes...

Japanese: 歯切れも往生ぎわも思いきりも、 何もかもが悪すぎます！
English: ...And here comes the backpedal!

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、センパイ。 この新米に、証明してください。
English: Come on now, "Rookie Killer"! Show this rookie how it's done!

Japanese: どのていどの覚悟をもって、 証人を“信頼”していたのか！
English: How much trust do you really have for this woman?

Japanese: あなたが“かけていた”と言う イノチが、どれほどのものなのか！
English: Are you willing to bet your life!?

Japanese: う‥‥うううう‥‥ううううううう
English: Gggg... Nnngh... Unnngggggh...

Japanese: うわあががあああがががあああがが あがあがあがあああああががががが
English: NNNNNNYYYYAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

Japanese: 毛が毛が毛が毛が毛が毛が毛が毛が 抜ける抜ける抜ける抜ける抜ける
English: M-M-My HAAAAAAAAAAIIIIRRR!! IT-IT-IT'S FLYYYYYIIIIIIIING OOOOOFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF!!

Japanese: 抜け落ちてゆくぞおおおおおおおお おおおオオオオオオオオオオオオオ
English: MY BEAUUUUUUUTIFUL HAIR!! NOOOOOOO!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: おおおおおおオオオオオオオおオ おおぉぉぉォォォォぉぉぉォ‥‥
English: WAAAAAHAAAAWAAAAHAAAA NO-NO-NO-NO-No-No-no-no noooooooooooooooooooooo...

Japanese: ‥‥それで、亜内検事。 美柳　ちなみは？
English: Umm, Mr. Payne? About Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne...?

Japanese: は、はッ！　先ほど、 緊急逮捕の手つづきを‥‥。
English: Y-Y-Yes, Your Honor! I'll file papers for her immediate arrest...

Japanese: ふう‥‥やれやれ、ですな。
English: Hmmm... Tragic... but not surprising.

Japanese: だいたい、最初から アヤしいと思っていました、私は！
English: I knew there was something suspicious about her from the very beginning!

Japanese: （‥‥ウソおっしゃい！）
English: (Don't lie! Just admit you were wrong!)

Japanese: ところで、弁護人？
English: By the way, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: は、はい！ なんでしょう？
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたは、美柳　ちなみを 知っていたようですが‥‥？
English: You said earlier that you and Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne knew each other...?

Japanese: それは‥‥本件とは 関係のないことです。
English: Your Honor... How we knew each other had nothing to do with this case.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 そうですか、わかりました。
English: Hmmm... Very well...

Japanese: ‥‥ええと、亜内検事。
English: Err, Mr. Payne.

Japanese: アクムだ！　これはユメなんだ！ この亜内が、自分のムスメみたいな
English: This can't be happening! It's a nightmare! It's like losing to my daughter!

Japanese: ‥‥カミの毛とタマシイが 抜けきってしまったようですな。
English: ...It appears Mr. Payne has lost his spirit along with his hair.

Japanese: しからば、被告人。
English: Does the defendant have anything further to say?

Japanese: ‥‥うう‥‥ウソだ‥‥ ちいちゃん‥‥うええ‥‥げほっ。
English: It-It can't be true... My... Dear... Dollie... *achoo*

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Hmm... Very well then...

Japanese: そろそろ、この法廷も 終わらせたほうがいいようですな。
English: I believe I am ready to pass judgment and bring this trial to an end.

Japanese: では、被告人・成歩堂　龍一に 判決を言いわたしましょう！
English: The court finds the defendant, Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: This court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　３時１６分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: April 11, 3:16 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: 千尋クン！ もお、スバラシかったぞい！
English: Mia! You were wonderful in there!

Japanese: ありがとうございます。 星影先生。
English: Thank you for everything, Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: 判決の瞬間、ワシのシリも わななき、ヒリつきおったワイ！
English: During the verdict, I thought my hemorrhoids were going to explode like Mt. Vesuvius!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。何かとおシリを 持ち出すの、やめません？
English: Umm, Mr. Grossberg... Do you, um, maybe think you could stop talking about them?

Japanese: うむ！　これはシツレイ。
English: Hmph! That's rather rude.

Japanese: とにかく、今日はワシも 考えさせられたぞ。
English: Anyway, this case really made me think.

Japanese: 依頼人との“信頼関係”。 そのコトバの、本当のイミを‥‥
English: What does it really mean to have a relationship of mutual trust with the client?

Japanese: 見失っているのは、ワレワレ ベテランのほうかもしれんのお。
English: Perhaps it is we veteran lawyers who have lost sight of this.

Japanese: あ‥‥ おめでとう！　なるほどくん。
English: Oh! Mr. Wright...! Congratulations!

Japanese: あの‥‥。ボク、 思ったんですけど‥‥
English: Th-Thanks... Um, you know... I was thinking...

Japanese: ‥‥？　なにかしら。
English: Go on...

Japanese: 今日のちいちゃん、本当に ホンモノだったんでしょうか！
English: The Dollie that I saw up there on the witness stand...
I don't think that was really her.

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: Um, what?

Japanese: ボクのちいちゃん、あんな ヒドいコト、言う子じゃないし‥‥
English: Yeah... The Dollie I know could NEVER have said those kinds of terrible things...

Japanese: もしかしたら、 よくできたニセモノ‥‥
English: Maybe... Maybe she was like... I don't know... A fake or something.

Japanese: （うう‥‥まだ、やられ足りないの かしら、この子は‥‥）
English: (Boy... This poor kid still hasn't got a clue...)

Japanese: 彼女のことは忘れなさい。 ‥‥それが、イチバンいいわ。
English: You need to forget about her, Mr. Wright. For your own sake...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥そう、ですよね。 それしかないんですよね。
English: Yeah, you're right... That's probably for the best.

Japanese: それから‥‥もう少し落ちついて。 オトナになりなさい。
English: Also... You need to relax a bit more. Try to grow up a little.

Japanese: え！　‥‥でも、ぼく！
English: B-But...

Japanese: トモダチの中じゃあ、いちばん オトナだって言われてるんですよ！
English: Out of all my friends, everyone says I'm the most grown up!

Japanese: （どんなトモダチづきあいを してるのかしら‥‥）
English: (Eek! What kind of company must this guy keep!?)

Japanese: ぼく‥‥今、弁護士を 目指して、勉強しているんです。
English: Right now I... I'm studying to become a lawyer myself.

Japanese: そうらしいわね。
English: That's what you keep saying...

Japanese: でも、あなた‥‥ たしか、芸術学部だったんじゃあ？
English: But I thought you were in the Art Department?

Japanese: え、ええ。まあ、 そうなんですけど‥‥。
English: Well, yeah... I guess I am...

Japanese: どうしても、助けたい 友だちがいるから。
English: But there's a friend that I desperately want to help!

Japanese: 急げば‥‥今なら、 まだ間に合うはずなんです！
English: And if I hurry, then I should still be able to save him in time!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなの。
English: I see.

Japanese: ねえ、千尋さん。
English: Say, Miss Fey?

Japanese: 弁護士は‥‥困ってるヒトを 救うことができるんですよね？
English: A lawyer is someone who can help people when they're in trouble, right?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 私も、まだまだ新米なのよ。
English: Mr. Wright, I'm still new at this myself.

Japanese: でも‥‥私は、そう信じてるわ。
English: But... I think that's exactly what a lawyer is.

Japanese: ぼく‥‥勉強します。 かならず、弁護士になるから！
English: OK... I'm going to do it. I'll study my butt off. I'll become a lawyer for sure!

Japanese: だから‥‥またいつか。 法廷で会おうね、千尋さん！
English: I hope... I hope we see each other again some day, maybe even in court.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが無罪判決を受けてから、 ５年の月日が流れた。
English: It's been five years since I was acquitted of all charges.

Japanese: ぼくは弁護士になって、 なんとか友だちを救い出し‥‥、
English: I became a lawyer like I planned, and managed to save my friend.

Japanese: 千尋さんは、この世を去った。
English: But Mia has passed on to a better place.

Japanese: この事件は、ぼくにとって ツラい思い出だ。
English: For me, this trial brings up a lot of painful memories.

Japanese: そして‥‥それと同時に、 とても大切な思い出でもある。
English: But... it also brings up some very precious ones.

Japanese: ‥‥もう二度と、ふり返ることは ないと思っていた記憶‥‥
English: And memories that I thought would never rise to the surface again...

Japanese: 千尋さんは、もういない。
English: Mia is gone now.

Japanese: でも‥‥。彼女はいつでも、 ぼくに語りかけてくれる。
English: But even so... I can hear her in my mind.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 何があっても、依頼人を信じるの。
English: Phoenix, no matter what, always believe in your client.

Japanese: 法廷で最後に必要になるのは、 ‥‥そのチカラよ。
English: In a court of law, your greatest weapon is your belief.

Japanese: あの事件から５年たって‥‥
English: ...Five long years...

Japanese: 彼女のコトバを、ぼくはもう一度、 噛みしめることになるのだが‥‥
English: Something has happened that's made me think back to her words of wisdom...

Japanese: ‥‥それはまだ、少し先の話だ。
English: But that is a story for another day...

Japanese: ■第１話　思い出の逆転 おわり
English: Episode 1: Turnabout Memories THE END
